questions.q: In which year was British India partitioned?
questions.explanation: The partition of British India took place in 1947, alongside independence.

questions.q: The partition of British India created India and which other nation?
questions.explanation: Partition divided British India into the two new states of India and Pakistan.

questions.q: Partition triggered one of the largest what in human history?
questions.explanation: Partition caused one of the largest mass migrations in history as millions crossed the new borders.

questions.q: Partition was accompanied by widespread what?
questions.explanation: The division was accompanied by widespread communal violence between communities.

questions.q: Which two provinces were physically divided during partition?
questions.explanation: Both Punjab and Bengal were split between India and Pakistan, causing enormous upheaval.

questions.q: Partition happened in the same year India became what?
questions.explanation: Partition coincided with India gaining independence in 1947.

questions.q: Which official drew the boundary line of partition?
questions.explanation: Sir Cyril Radcliffe chaired the boundary commission that drew the partition line.

questions.q: The new state created for Muslims at partition was called what?
questions.explanation: Pakistan was created as a separate state at partition.

questions.q: Partition is sometimes remembered as 'The Great' what?
questions.explanation: Partition is often called 'The Great Divide' for the immense rupture it caused.

questions.q: At its creation in 1947, Pakistan was made up of how many separated wings?
questions.explanation: Pakistan was formed of two wings, West and East Pakistan, separated by Indian territory.

questions.q: Roughly how many people are estimated to have migrated across the new borders during partition?
questions.explanation: An estimated 10 to 15 million people crossed the borders in the partition migrations.

questions.q: The eastern wing of Pakistan in 1947 was carved out of which province?
questions.explanation: East Pakistan was formed from the eastern, Muslim-majority part of Bengal.

questions.q: Which leader of the Muslim League is most associated with the demand that led to partition?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah led the Muslim League's demand for a separate Muslim homeland.

questions.q: The 1940 resolution that formally demanded separate Muslim states was passed at which city?
questions.explanation: The Lahore Resolution of 1940 articulated the demand that culminated in partition.

questions.q: The boundaries of partition were published at what point relative to independence?
questions.explanation: The Radcliffe boundary award was published only after the independence celebrations, adding to the chaos.

questions.q: Which region's status was left unresolved at partition and became a long-running dispute?
questions.explanation: The princely state of Jammu and Kashmir's accession was unresolved at partition, sparking lasting conflict.

questions.q: Which Indian leader was deeply opposed to partition and called it a vivisection of the country?
questions.explanation: Gandhi opposed partition bitterly, describing the division of India as a vivisection.

questions.q: Partition led to the displacement of communities primarily along which lines?
questions.explanation: Migration during partition broadly followed religious lines, with Hindus and Sikhs and Muslims moving in opposite directions.

questions.q: Which city in West Punjab, a centre of culture, became part of Pakistan at partition?
questions.explanation: Lahore, a major cultural city, went to Pakistan, while nearby Amritsar remained in India.

questions.q: Who became the first Governor-General of Pakistan after partition?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah became the first Governor-General of the new state of Pakistan.

questions.q: Estimates of the death toll from partition violence commonly range up to about how many?
options: Several hundred thousand to a million or more
questions.explanation: Scholarly estimates of partition deaths vary widely, commonly ranging from several hundred thousand up to a million or more.

questions.q: Partition is closely tied to which 1947 plan that announced the division of British India?
questions.explanation: The Mountbatten Plan of June 1947 set out the partition into two dominions.

questions.q: The two boundary commissions Radcliffe chaired dealt with the partition of which two provinces?
questions.explanation: Radcliffe chaired separate boundary commissions for Punjab and for Bengal.

questions.q: Cyril Radcliffe had what notable disqualifying background for the boundary task?
options: He had lived in India for decades
He had never visited India before
questions.explanation: Radcliffe had never set foot in India before being given just weeks to draw its borders.

questions.q: The disputed allocation of which district to India inflamed Punjab boundary controversies?
questions.explanation: The award of Gurdaspur to India, giving access toward Kashmir, became one of the most contested boundary decisions.

questions.q: Partition created an acute administrative problem by also dividing what between the two states?
questions.explanation: The armed forces, financial assets and government machinery all had to be divided between India and Pakistan.

questions.q: Which border region saw some of the worst massacres and refugee columns during partition?
questions.explanation: Punjab, split between the two countries, witnessed the most intense violence and the largest refugee movements.

questions.q: Abduction and recovery of women on both sides led the two governments to launch what joint effort?
options: An inter-dominion recovery operation for abducted women
questions.explanation: India and Pakistan undertook a joint operation to recover and repatriate the many women abducted during partition violence.

questions.q: The eastern wing created at partition, East Pakistan, later became which independent country?
questions.explanation: East Pakistan broke away in 1971 to become the independent nation of Bangladesh.

questions.q: Why do historians often describe the Radcliffe boundary process as deeply flawed?
options: It was rushed, secretive and ignored ground realities
It was decided by public vote
questions.explanation: The boundary was drawn hurriedly in secret with little local knowledge, contributing to confusion, displacement and bloodshed.
